decision_point: In an active patient undergoing total knee arthroplasty, is cemented or cementless implant fixation preferred?
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: In an active patient undergoing total knee arthroplasty, is cemented or cementless implant fixation preferred?
Options:
  1. Cemented implant fixation
  2. Cementless implant fixation
label: evidence_split
label_rationale: Cemented TKA is the historic gold standard with reliable, long-term survival; cementless TKA utilizes biological ingrowth to preserve bone but has higher component costs and rare early loosening.

reviews.notes: which-option label stratum via llm_haiku — Cemented TKA remains the gold standard with proven long-term survival, but cementless designs show comparable outcomes in selected active patients with potential bone-preservation benefits, creating genuine literature conflict without a famous public debate.

auditor reasoning: Genuine long-term (20yr+) uncertainty remains (keep evidence_split), but this is one of the loudest, most heavily marketed AAHKS/AAOS debates — stratum only. | Claude second opinion: Agree. Real uncertainty remains on 20+ year cementless data, and this is genuinely one of the more heavily marketed AAHKS/AAOS debates -- editorialized fits.
